diff --git a/receiver/oracledbreceiver/metadata.yaml b/receiver/oracledbreceiver/metadata.yaml
index d23b279a8f..d4d056127e 100644
--- a/receiver/oracledbreceiver/metadata.yaml
+++ b/receiver/oracledbreceiver/metadata.yaml
@@ -593,6 +593,184 @@ metrics:
       input_type: string
     unit: "{operation}"
 
+  # Query-Level Metrics
+  oracledb.query.executions:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The number of times a specific SQL query has been executed.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{executions}"
+  oracledb.query.elapsed_time:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The total time (in seconds) taken by a query from start to finish, including CPU time and all types of waits.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: double
+    unit: "s"
+  oracledb.query.cpu_time:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: Total time (in seconds) that the CPU spent actively processing a query, excluding time spent waiting.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: double
+    unit: "s"
+  oracledb.query.application_wait_time:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting on the application before it could proceed with execution.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: double
+    unit: "s"
+  oracledb.query.concurrency_wait_time:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: Total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting on concurrency-related events.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: double
+    unit: "s"
+  oracledb.query.user_io_wait_time:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting for user I/O operations.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: double
+    unit: "s"
+  oracledb.query.cluster_wait_time:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: Total time (in seconds) that a query waited due to Oracle Real Application Clusters (RAC) coordination.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: double
+    unit: "s"
+  oracledb.query.rows_processed:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The total number of rows that a query has read, returned, or affected during its execution.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{rows}"
+  oracledb.query.buffer_gets:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: Number of logical reads (i.e., buffer cache accesses) performed by a query.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{gets}"
+  oracledb.query.physical_read_requests:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The number of physical I/O read operations performed by a query.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{requests}"
+  oracledb.query.physical_read_bytes:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The total number of bytes read from disk by a query.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "By"
+  oracledb.query.physical_write_requests:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The number of times a query requested to write data to disk.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{requests}"
+  oracledb.query.physical_write_bytes:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The total number of bytes written to disk by a query.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "By"
+  oracledb.query.disk_reads:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The number of physical reads a query performs — that is, the number of data blocks read from disk.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{reads}"
+  oracledb.query.direct_reads:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The number of direct path reads performed by a query — i.e., data blocks read directly from disk into the session’s memory.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{reads}"
+  oracledb.query.direct_writes:
+    attributes:
+      - oracledb.sql_id
+      - oracledb.child_number
+    description: The number of direct path write operations, where data is written directly to disk from user memory.
+    enabled: false
+    sum:
+      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
+      monotonic: true
+      value_type: int
+    unit: "{writes}"
+
 
 tests:
   config:
diff --git a/receiver/oracledbreceiver/scraper.go b/receiver/oracledbreceiver/scraper.go
index 9e0dd2cb34..3a8cbd0c3c 100644
--- a/receiver/oracledbreceiver/scraper.go
+++ b/receiver/oracledbreceiver/scraper.go
@@ -131,6 +131,7 @@ type oracleScraper struct {
 	topQueryCollectCfg         TopQueryCollection
 	obfuscator                 *obfuscator
 	querySampleCfg             QuerySample
+	queryLevelMetricCache      *queryLevelMetricCache
 }
 
 func newScraper(metricsBuilder *metadata.MetricsBuilder, metricsBuilderConfig metadata.MetricsBuilderConfig, scrapeCfg scraperhelper.ControllerConfig, logger *zap.Logger, providerFunc dbProviderFunc, clientProviderFunc clientProviderFunc, instanceName, hostName string) (scraper.Metrics, error) {
@@ -502,6 +503,36 @@ func (s *oracleScraper) scrape(ctx context.Context) (pmetric.Metrics, error) {
 		}
 	}
 
+	// Collect query-level metrics if any are enabled
+	runQueryLevelMetrics := s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryExecutions.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryElapsedTime.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryCpuTime.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryApplicationWaitTime.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryConcurrencyWaitTime.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryUserIoWaitTime.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryClusterWaitTime.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryRowsProcessed.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryBufferGets.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryPhysicalReadRequests.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryPhysicalReadBytes.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryPhysicalWriteRequests.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryPhysicalWriteBytes.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryDiskReads.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryDirectReads.Enabled ||
+		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueryDirectWrites.Enabled
+
+	if runQueryLevelMetrics {
+		s.ensureQueryLevelMetricCache()
+		now := pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now())
+		queryMetrics, err := s.collectQueryLevelMetrics(ctx)
+		if err != nil {
+			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("error collecting query-level metrics: %w", err))
+		} else if len(queryMetrics) > 0 {
+			s.recordQueryLevelMetrics(now, queryMetrics)
+			s.logger.Debug("Recorded query-level metrics", zap.Int("queries", len(queryMetrics)))
+		}
+	}
+
 	rb := s.mb.NewResourceBuilder()
 	rb.SetOracledbInstanceName(s.instanceName)
 	rb.SetHostName(s.hostName)

     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"context"
     8→	"database/sql"
     9→	_ "embed"
    10→	"encoding/hex"
    11→	"encoding/json"
    12→	"errors"
    13→	"fmt"
    14→	"sort"
    15→	"strconv"
    16→	"strings"
    17→	"time"
    18→
    19→	lru "github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2"
    20→	"go.opentelemetry.io/collector/component"
    21→	"go.opentelemetry.io/collector/pdata/pcommon"
    22→	"go.opentelemetry.io/collector/pdata/plog"
    23→	"go.opentelemetry.io/collector/pdata/pmetric"
    24→	"go.opentelemetry.io/collector/scraper"

    32→)
    33→
    34→const (
    35→	statsSQL                       = "select * from v$sysstat"
    36→	enqueueDeadlocks               = "enqueue deadlocks"
    37→	exchangeDeadlocks              = "exchange deadlocks"
    38→	executeCount                   = "execute count"
    39→	parseCountTotal                = "parse count (total)"
    40→	parseCountHard                 = "parse count (hard)"
    41→	logons                         = "logons cumulative"
    42→	userCommits                    = "user commits"
    43→	userRollbacks                  = "user rollbacks"
    44→	physicalReads                  = "physical reads"
    45→	physicalReadsDirect            = "physical reads direct"
    46→	physicalReadIORequests         = "physical read IO requests"
    47→	physicalWrites                 = "physical writes"
    48→	physicalWritesDirect           = "physical writes direct"
    49→	physicalWriteIORequests        = "physical write IO requests"
    50→	queriesParallelized            = "queries parallelized"
    51→	ddlStatementsParallelized      = "DDL statements parallelized"
    52→	dmlStatementsParallelized      = "DML statements parallelized"
    53→	parallelOpsNotDowngraded       = "Parallel operations not downgraded"
    54→	parallelOpsDowngradedToSerial  = "Parallel operations downgraded to serial"
    55→	parallelOpsDowngraded1To25Pct  = "Parallel operations downgraded 1 to 25 pct"
    56→	parallelOpsDowngraded25To50Pct = "Parallel operations downgraded 25 to 50 pct"
    57→	parallelOpsDowngraded50To75Pct = "Parallel operations downgraded 50 to 75 pct"
    58→	parallelOpsDowngraded75To99Pct = "Parallel operations downgraded 75 to 99 pct"
    59→	sessionLogicalReads            = "session logical reads"
    60→	cpuTime                        = "CPU used by this session"
    61→	pgaMemory                      = "session pga memory"
    62→	dbBlockGets                    = "db block gets"
    63→	consistentGets                 = "consistent gets"
    64→	sessionCountSQL                = "select status, type, count(*) as VALUE FROM v$session GROUP BY status, type"
    65→	systemResourceLimitsSQL        = "select RESOURCE_NAME, CURRENT_UTILIZATION, LIMIT_VALUE, CASE WHEN TRIM(INITIAL_ALLOCATION) LIKE 'UNLIMITED' THEN '-1' ELSE TRIM(INITIAL_ALLOCATION) END as INITIAL_ALLOCATION, CASE WHEN TRIM(LIMIT_VALUE) LIKE 'UNLIMITED' THEN '-1' ELSE TRIM(LIMIT_VALUE) END as LIMIT_VALUE from v$resource_limit"
    66→	tablespaceUsageSQL             = `
    67→		select um.TABLESPACE_NAME, um.USED_SPACE, um.TABLESPACE_SIZE, ts.BLOCK_SIZE
    68→		FROM DBA_TABLESPACE_USAGE_METRICS um INNER JOIN DBA_TABLESPACES ts
    69→		ON um.TABLESPACE_NAME = ts.TABLESPACE_NAME`
    70→
    71→	dbTimeReferenceFormat = "2006-01-02 15:04:05"
    72→	sqlIDAttr             = "SQL_ID"
    73→	childAddressAttr      = "CHILD_ADDRESS"
    74→	childNumberAttr       = "CHILD_NUMBER"
    75→	sqlTextAttr           = "SQL_FULLTEXT"
    76→	dbSystemNameVal       = "oracle"
    77→
    78→	queryExecutionMetric        = "EXECUTIONS"
    79→	elapsedTimeMetric           = "ELAPSED_TIME"
    80→	cpuTimeMetric               = "CPU_TIME"
    81→	applicationWaitTimeMetric   = "APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME"
    82→	concurrencyWaitTimeMetric   = "CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME"
    83→	userIoWaitTimeMetric        = "USER_IO_WAIT_TIME"
    84→	clusterWaitTimeMetric       = "CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME"
    85→	rowsProcessedMetric         = "ROWS_PROCESSED"
    86→	bufferGetsMetric            = "BUFFER_GETS"
    87→	physicalReadRequestsMetric  = "PHYSICAL_READ_REQUESTS"
    88→	physicalWriteRequestsMetric = "PHYSICAL_WRITE_REQUESTS"
    89→	physicalReadBytesMetric     = "PHYSICAL_READ_BYTES"
    90→	physicalWriteBytesMetric    = "PHYSICAL_WRITE_BYTES"
    91→	queryDiskReadsMetric        = "DISK_READS"
    92→	queryDirectReadsMetric      = "DIRECT_READS"
    93→	queryDirectWritesMetric     = "DIRECT_WRITES"
    94→)
    95→
    96→var (
    97→	//go:embed templates/oracleQuerySampleSql.tmpl
    98→	samplesQuery string
    99→	//go:embed templates/oracleQueryMetricsAndTextSql.tmpl
   100→	oracleQueryMetricsSQL string
   101→	//go:embed templates/oracleQueryPlanSql.tmpl
   102→	oracleQueryPlanDataSQL string
   103→)
   104→
   105→type dbProviderFunc func() (*sql.DB, error)
   106→
   107→type clientProviderFunc func(*sql.DB, string, *zap.Logger) dbClient
   108→
   109→type oracleScraper struct {
   110→	statsClient                dbClient
   111→	tablespaceUsageClient      dbClient
   112→	systemResourceLimitsClient dbClient
   113→	sessionCountClient         dbClient
   114→	oracleQueryMetricsClient   dbClient
   115→	oraclePlanDataClient       dbClient
   116→	samplesQueryClient         dbClient
   117→	db                         *sql.DB
   118→	clientProviderFunc         clientProviderFunc
   119→	mb                         *metadata.MetricsBuilder
   120→	lb                         *metadata.LogsBuilder
   121→	dbProviderFunc             dbProviderFunc
   122→	logger                     *zap.Logger
   123→	id                         component.ID
   124→	instanceName               string
   125→	hostName                   string
   126→	scrapeCfg                  scraperhelper.ControllerConfig
   127→	startTime                  pcommon.Timestamp
   128→	metricsBuilderConfig       metadata.MetricsBuilderConfig
   129→	logsBuilderConfig          metadata.LogsBuilderConfig
   130→	metricCache                *lru.Cache[string, map[string]int64]
   131→	topQueryCollectCfg         TopQueryCollection
   132→	obfuscator                 *obfuscator
   133→	querySampleCfg             QuerySample
   134→}
   135→
   136→func newScraper(metricsBuilder *metadata.MetricsBuilder, metricsBuilderConfig metadata.MetricsBuilderConfig, scrapeCfg scraperhelper.ControllerConfig, logger *zap.Logger, providerFunc dbProviderFunc, clientProviderFunc clientProviderFunc, instanceName, hostName string) (scraper.Metrics, error) {
   137→	s := &oracleScraper{
   138→		mb:                   metricsBuilder,
   139→		metricsBuilderConfig: metricsBuilderConfig,
   140→		scrapeCfg:            scrapeCfg,
   141→		logger:               logger,
   142→		dbProviderFunc:       providerFunc,
   143→		clientProviderFunc:   clientProviderFunc,
   144→		instanceName:         instanceName,
   145→		hostName:             hostName,
   146→	}
   147→	return scraper.NewMetrics(s.scrape, scraper.WithShutdown(s.shutdown), scraper.WithStart(s.start))
   148→}
   149→
   150→func newLogsScraper(logsBuilder *metadata.LogsBuilder, logsBuilderConfig metadata.LogsBuilderConfig, scrapeCfg scraperhelper.ControllerConfig,
   151→	logger *zap.Logger, providerFunc dbProviderFunc, clientProviderFunc clientProviderFunc, instanceName string, metricCache *lru.Cache[string, map[string]int64],
   152→	topQueryCollectCfg TopQueryCollection, querySampleCfg QuerySample, hostName string,
   153→) (scraper.Logs, error) {
   154→	s := &oracleScraper{
   155→		lb:                 logsBuilder,
   156→		logsBuilderConfig:  logsBuilderConfig,
   157→		scrapeCfg:          scrapeCfg,
   158→		logger:             logger,
   159→		dbProviderFunc:     providerFunc,
   160→		clientProviderFunc: clientProviderFunc,
   161→		instanceName:       instanceName,
   162→		metricCache:        metricCache,
   163→		topQueryCollectCfg: topQueryCollectCfg,
   164→		querySampleCfg:     querySampleCfg,
   165→		hostName:           hostName,
   166→		obfuscator:         newObfuscator(),
   167→	}
   168→	return scraper.NewLogs(s.scrapeLogs, scraper.WithShutdown(s.shutdown), scraper.WithStart(s.start))
   169→}
   170→
   171→func (s *oracleScraper) start(context.Context, component.Host) error {
   172→	s.startTime = pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now())
   173→	var err error
   174→	s.db, err = s.dbProviderFunc()
   175→	if err != nil {
   176→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to open db connection: %w", err)
   177→	}
   178→	s.statsClient = s.clientProviderFunc(s.db, statsSQL, s.logger)
   179→	s.sessionCountClient = s.clientProviderFunc(s.db, sessionCountSQL, s.logger)
   180→	s.systemResourceLimitsClient = s.clientProviderFunc(s.db, systemResourceLimitsSQL, s.logger)
   181→	s.tablespaceUsageClient = s.clientProviderFunc(s.db, tablespaceUsageSQL, s.logger)
   182→	s.samplesQueryClient = s.clientProviderFunc(s.db, samplesQuery, s.logger)
   183→	return nil
   184→}
   185→
   186→func (s *oracleScraper) scrape(ctx context.Context) (pmetric.Metrics, error) {
   187→	s.logger.Debug("Begin scrape")
   188→
   189→	var scrapeErrors []error
   190→
   191→	runStats := s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbEnqueueDeadlocks.Enabled ||
   192→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbExchangeDeadlocks.Enabled ||
   193→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbExecutions.Enabled ||
   194→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbParseCalls.Enabled ||
   195→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbHardParses.Enabled ||
   196→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbUserCommits.Enabled ||
   197→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbUserRollbacks.Enabled ||
   198→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbPhysicalReads.Enabled ||
   199→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbPhysicalReadsDirect.Enabled ||
   200→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbPhysicalReadIoRequests.Enabled ||
   201→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbPhysicalWrites.Enabled ||
   202→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbPhysicalWritesDirect.Enabled ||
   203→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbPhysicalWriteIoRequests.Enabled ||
   204→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbQueriesParallelized.Enabled ||
   205→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbDdlStatementsParallelized.Enabled ||
   206→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbDmlStatementsParallelized.Enabled ||
   207→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbParallelOperationsNotDowngraded.Enabled ||
   208→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbParallelOperationsDowngradedToSerial.Enabled ||
   209→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded1To25Pct.Enabled ||
   210→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded25To50Pct.Enabled ||
   211→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded50To75Pct.Enabled ||
   212→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded75To99Pct.Enabled ||
   213→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbLogicalReads.Enabled ||
   214→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbCPUTime.Enabled ||
   215→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbPgaMemory.Enabled ||
   216→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbDbBlockGets.Enabled ||
   217→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbConsistentGets.Enabled ||
   218→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbLogons.Enabled
   219→	if runStats {
   220→		now := pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now())
   221→		rows, execError := s.statsClient.metricRows(ctx)
   222→		if execError != nil {
   223→			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("error executing %s: %w", statsSQL, execError))
   224→		}
   225→
   226→		for _, row := range rows {
   227→			switch row["NAME"] {
   228→			case enqueueDeadlocks:
   229→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbEnqueueDeadlocksDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   230→				if err != nil {
   231→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   232→				}
   233→			case exchangeDeadlocks:
   234→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbExchangeDeadlocksDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   235→				if err != nil {
   236→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   237→				}
   238→			case executeCount:
   239→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbExecutionsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   240→				if err != nil {
   241→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   242→				}
   243→			case parseCountTotal:
   244→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbParseCallsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   245→				if err != nil {
   246→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   247→				}
   248→			case parseCountHard:
   249→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbHardParsesDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   250→				if err != nil {
   251→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   252→				}
   253→			case userCommits:
   254→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbUserCommitsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   255→				if err != nil {
   256→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   257→				}
   258→			case userRollbacks:
   259→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbUserRollbacksDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   260→				if err != nil {
   261→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   262→				}
   263→			case physicalReads:
   264→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbPhysicalReadsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   265→				if err != nil {
   266→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   267→				}
   268→			case physicalReadsDirect:
   269→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbPhysicalReadsDirectDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   270→				if err != nil {
   271→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   272→				}
   273→			case physicalReadIORequests:
   274→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbPhysicalReadIoRequestsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   275→				if err != nil {
   276→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   277→				}
   278→			case physicalWrites:
   279→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbPhysicalWritesDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   280→				if err != nil {
   281→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   282→				}
   283→			case physicalWritesDirect:
   284→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbPhysicalWritesDirectDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   285→				if err != nil {
   286→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   287→				}
   288→			case physicalWriteIORequests:
   289→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbPhysicalWriteIoRequestsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   290→				if err != nil {
   291→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   292→				}
   293→			case queriesParallelized:
   294→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbQueriesParallelizedDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   295→				if err != nil {
   296→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   297→				}
   298→			case ddlStatementsParallelized:
   299→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbDdlStatementsParallelizedDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   300→				if err != nil {
   301→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   302→				}
   303→			case dmlStatementsParallelized:
   304→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbDmlStatementsParallelizedDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   305→				if err != nil {
   306→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   307→				}
   308→			case parallelOpsNotDowngraded:
   309→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbParallelOperationsNotDowngradedDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   310→				if err != nil {
   311→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   312→				}
   313→			case parallelOpsDowngradedToSerial:
   314→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbParallelOperationsDowngradedToSerialDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   315→				if err != nil {
   316→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   317→				}
   318→			case parallelOpsDowngraded1To25Pct:
   319→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded1To25PctDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   320→				if err != nil {
   321→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   322→				}
   323→			case parallelOpsDowngraded25To50Pct:
   324→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded25To50PctDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   325→				if err != nil {
   326→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   327→				}
   328→			case parallelOpsDowngraded50To75Pct:
   329→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded50To75PctDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   330→				if err != nil {
   331→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   332→				}
   333→			case parallelOpsDowngraded75To99Pct:
   334→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbParallelOperationsDowngraded75To99PctDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   335→				if err != nil {
   336→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   337→				}
   338→			case sessionLogicalReads:
   339→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbLogicalReadsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   340→				if err != nil {
   341→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   342→				}
   343→			case cpuTime:
   344→				value, err := strconv.ParseFloat(row["VALUE"], 64)
   345→				if err != nil {
   346→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("%s value: %q, %w", cpuTime, row["VALUE"], err))
   347→				} else {
   348→					// divide by 100 as the value is expressed in tens of milliseconds
   349→					value /= 100
   350→					s.mb.RecordOracledbCPUTimeDataPoint(now, value)
   351→				}
   352→			case pgaMemory:
   353→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbPgaMemoryDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()), row["VALUE"])
   354→				if err != nil {
   355→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   356→				}
   357→			case dbBlockGets:
   358→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbDbBlockGetsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   359→				if err != nil {
   360→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   361→				}
   362→			case consistentGets:
   363→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbConsistentGetsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   364→				if err != nil {
   365→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   366→				}
   367→			case logons:
   368→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbLogonsDataPoint(now, row["VALUE"])
   369→				if err != nil {
   370→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   371→				}
   372→			}
   373→		}
   374→	}
   375→
   376→	if s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbSessionsUsage.Enabled {
   377→		rows, err := s.sessionCountClient.metricRows(ctx)
   378→		if err != nil {
   379→			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("error executing %s: %w", sessionCountSQL, err))
   380→		}
   381→		for _, row := range rows {
   382→			err := s.mb.RecordOracledbSessionsUsageDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()), row["VALUE"],
   383→				row["TYPE"], row["STATUS"])
   384→			if err != nil {
   385→				scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   386→			}
   387→		}
   388→	}
   389→
   390→	if s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbSessionsLimit.Enabled ||
   391→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbProcessesUsage.Enabled ||
   392→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbProcessesLimit.Enabled ||
   393→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbEnqueueResourcesUsage.Enabled ||
   394→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbEnqueueResourcesLimit.Enabled ||
   395→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbEnqueueLocksLimit.Enabled ||
   396→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbEnqueueLocksUsage.Enabled {
   397→		rows, err := s.systemResourceLimitsClient.metricRows(ctx)
   398→		if err != nil {
   399→			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("error executing %s: %w", systemResourceLimitsSQL, err))
   400→		}
   401→		for _, row := range rows {
   402→			resourceName := row["RESOURCE_NAME"]
   403→			switch resourceName {
   404→			case "processes":
   405→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbProcessesUsageDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   406→					row["CURRENT_UTILIZATION"]); err != nil {
   407→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   408→				}
   409→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbProcessesLimitDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   410→					row["LIMIT_VALUE"]); err != nil {
   411→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   412→				}
   413→			case "sessions":
   414→				err := s.mb.RecordOracledbSessionsLimitDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   415→					row["LIMIT_VALUE"])
   416→				if err != nil {
   417→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   418→				}
   419→			case "enqueue_locks":
   420→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbEnqueueLocksUsageDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   421→					row["CURRENT_UTILIZATION"]); err != nil {
   422→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   423→				}
   424→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbEnqueueLocksLimitDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   425→					row["LIMIT_VALUE"]); err != nil {
   426→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   427→				}
   428→			case "dml_locks":
   429→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbDmlLocksUsageDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   430→					row["CURRENT_UTILIZATION"]); err != nil {
   431→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   432→				}
   433→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbDmlLocksLimitDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   434→					row["LIMIT_VALUE"]); err != nil {
   435→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   436→				}
   437→			case "enqueue_resources":
   438→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbEnqueueResourcesUsageDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   439→					row["CURRENT_UTILIZATION"]); err != nil {
   440→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   441→				}
   442→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbEnqueueResourcesLimitDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   443→					row["LIMIT_VALUE"]); err != nil {
   444→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   445→				}
   446→			case "transactions":
   447→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbTransactionsUsageDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   448→					row["CURRENT_UTILIZATION"]); err != nil {
   449→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   450→				}
   451→				if err := s.mb.RecordOracledbTransactionsLimitDataPoint(pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now()),
   452→					row["LIMIT_VALUE"]); err != nil {
   453→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, err)
   454→				}
   455→			}
   456→		}
   457→	}
   458→
   459→	if s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbTablespaceSizeUsage.Enabled ||
   460→		s.metricsBuilderConfig.Metrics.OracledbTablespaceSizeLimit.Enabled {
   461→		rows, err := s.tablespaceUsageClient.metricRows(ctx)
   462→		if err != nil {
   463→			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("error executing %s: %w", tablespaceUsageSQL, err))
   464→		} else {
   465→			now := pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now())
   466→			for _, row := range rows {
   467→				tablespaceName := row["TABLESPACE_NAME"]
   468→				usedSpaceBlockCount, err := strconv.ParseInt(row["USED_SPACE"], 10, 64)
   469→				if err != nil {
   470→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse int64 for OracledbTablespaceSizeUsage, value was %s: %w", row["USED_SPACE"], err))
   471→					continue
   472→				}
   473→
   474→				tablespaceSizeOriginal := row["TABLESPACE_SIZE"]
   475→				var tablespaceSizeBlockCount int64
   476→				// Tablespace size should never be empty using the DBA_TABLESPACE_USAGE_METRICS query. This logic is done
   477→				// to preserve backward compatibility for with the original metric gathered from querying DBA_TABLESPACES
   478→				if tablespaceSizeOriginal == "" {
   479→					tablespaceSizeBlockCount = -1
   480→				} else {
   481→					tablespaceSizeBlockCount, err = strconv.ParseInt(tablespaceSizeOriginal, 10, 64)
   482→					if err != nil {
   483→						scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse int64 for OracledbTablespaceSizeLimit, value was %s: %w", tablespaceSizeOriginal, err))
   484→						continue
   485→					}
   486→				}
   487→
   488→				blockSize, err := strconv.ParseInt(row["BLOCK_SIZE"], 10, 64)
   489→				if err != nil {
   490→					scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse int64 for OracledbBlockSize, value was %s: %w", row["BLOCK_SIZE"], err))
   491→					continue
   492→				}
   493→
   494→				s.mb.RecordOracledbTablespaceSizeUsageDataPoint(now, usedSpaceBlockCount*blockSize, tablespaceName)
   495→
   496→				if tablespaceSizeBlockCount < 0 {
   497→					s.mb.RecordOracledbTablespaceSizeLimitDataPoint(now, -1, tablespaceName)
   498→				} else {
   499→					s.mb.RecordOracledbTablespaceSizeLimitDataPoint(now, tablespaceSizeBlockCount*blockSize, tablespaceName)
   500→				}
   501→			}
   502→		}
   503→	}
   504→
   505→	rb := s.mb.NewResourceBuilder()
   506→	rb.SetOracledbInstanceName(s.instanceName)
   507→	rb.SetHostName(s.hostName)
   508→	out := s.mb.Emit(metadata.WithResource(rb.Emit()))
   509→	s.logger.Debug("Done scraping")
   510→	if len(scrapeErrors) > 0 {
   511→		return out, scrapererror.NewPartialScrapeError(multierr.Combine(scrapeErrors...), len(scrapeErrors))
   512→	}
   513→	return out, nil
   514→}
   515→
   516→type queryMetricCacheHit struct {
   517→	sqlID        string
   518→	childNumber  string
   519→	childAddress string
   520→	queryText    string
   521→	metrics      map[string]int64
   522→}
   523→
   524→func (s *oracleScraper) scrapeLogs(ctx context.Context) (plog.Logs, error) {
   525→	logs := plog.NewLogs()
   526→	var scrapeErrors []error
   527→
   528→	if s.logsBuilderConfig.Events.DbServerTopQuery.Enabled {
   529→		topNCollectionErrors := s.collectTopNMetricData(ctx, logs)
   530→		if topNCollectionErrors != nil {
   531→			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, topNCollectionErrors)
   532→		}
   533→	}
   534→
   535→	if s.logsBuilderConfig.Events.DbServerQuerySample.Enabled {
   536→		samplesCollectionErrors := s.collectQuerySamples(ctx, logs)
   537→		if samplesCollectionErrors != nil {
   538→			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, samplesCollectionErrors)
   539→		}
   540→	}
   541→
   542→	return logs, errors.Join(scrapeErrors...)
   543→}
   544→
   545→func (s *oracleScraper) collectTopNMetricData(ctx context.Context, logs plog.Logs) error {
   546→	var errs []error
   547→	// get metrics and query texts from DB
   548→	timestamp := pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now())
   549→	intervalSeconds := int(s.scrapeCfg.CollectionInterval.Seconds())
   550→	s.oracleQueryMetricsClient = s.clientProviderFunc(s.db, oracleQueryMetricsSQL, s.logger)
   551→	now := timestamp.AsTime().Format(dbTimeReferenceFormat)
   552→	metricRows, metricError := s.oracleQueryMetricsClient.metricRows(ctx, now, intervalSeconds, s.topQueryCollectCfg.MaxQuerySampleCount)
   553→
   554→	if metricError != nil {
   555→		return fmt.Errorf("error executing oracleQueryMetricsSQL: %w", metricError)
   556→	}
   557→	if len(metricRows) == 0 {
   558→		return errors.New("no data returned from oracleQueryMetricsClient")
   559→	}
   560→
   561→	metricNames := s.getTopNMetricNames()
   562→	var hits []queryMetricCacheHit
   563→	var cacheUpdates, discardedHits int
   564→	for _, row := range metricRows {
   565→		newCacheVal := make(map[string]int64, len(metricNames))
   566→		for _, columnName := range metricNames {
   567→			val := row[columnName]
   568→			valInt64, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64)
   569→			if err != nil {
   570→				errs = append(errs, err)
   571→			} else {
   572→				newCacheVal[columnName] = valInt64
   573→			}
   574→		}
   575→
   576→		cacheKey := fmt.Sprintf("%v:%v", row[sqlIDAttr], row[childNumberAttr])
   577→		// if we have a cache hit and the query doesn't belong to top N, cache is updated anyway
   578→		// as a result, once it finally makes its way to the top N queries, only the latest delta will be sent downstream
   579→		if oldCacheVal, ok := s.metricCache.Get(cacheKey); ok {
   580→			hit := queryMetricCacheHit{
   581→				sqlID:        row[sqlIDAttr],
   582→				queryText:    row[sqlTextAttr],
   583→				childNumber:  row[childNumberAttr],
   584→				childAddress: row[childAddressAttr],
   585→				metrics:      make(map[string]int64, len(metricNames)),
   586→			}
   587→
   588→			// it is possible we get a record with all deltas equal to zero. we don't want to process it any further
   589→			var possiblePurge, positiveDelta bool
   590→			for _, columnName := range metricNames {
   591→				delta := newCacheVal[columnName] - oldCacheVal[columnName]
   592→
   593→				// if any of the deltas is less than zero, cursor was likely purged from the shared pool
   594→				if delta < 0 {
   595→					possiblePurge = true
   596→					break
   597→				} else if delta > 0 {
   598→					positiveDelta = true
   599→				}
   600→
   601→				hit.metrics[columnName] = delta
   602→			}
   603→
   604→			// skip if possible purge or all the deltas are equal to zero
   605→			if !possiblePurge && positiveDelta {
   606→				hits = append(hits, hit)
   607→			} else {
   608→				discardedHits++
   609→			}
   610→		}
   611→		s.metricCache.Add(cacheKey, newCacheVal)
   612→		cacheUpdates++
   613→	}
   614→
   615→	// if cache updates is not equal to rows returned, that indicates there is problem somewhere
   616→	s.logger.Debug("Cache update", zap.Int("update-count", cacheUpdates), zap.Int("new-size", s.metricCache.Len()))
   617→
   618→	if len(hits) == 0 {
   619→		s.logger.Info("No log records for this scrape")
   620→		return errors.Join(errs...)
   621→	}
   622→
   623→	s.logger.Debug("Cache hits", zap.Int("hit-count", len(hits)), zap.Int("discarded-hit-count", discardedHits))
   624→
   625→	// order by elapsed time delta, descending
   626→	sort.Slice(hits, func(i, j int) bool {
   627→		return hits[i].metrics[elapsedTimeMetric] > hits[j].metrics[elapsedTimeMetric]
   628→	})
   629→
   630→	// keep at most maxHitSize
   631→	maxHitsSize := min(len(hits), int(s.topQueryCollectCfg.TopQueryCount))
   632→	hits = hits[:maxHitsSize]
   633→
   634→	hits = s.obfuscateCacheHits(hits)
   635→	childAddressToPlanMap := s.getChildAddressToPlanMap(ctx, hits)
   636→
   637→	rb := s.lb.NewResourceBuilder()
   638→	rb.SetOracledbInstanceName(s.instanceName)
   639→	rb.SetHostName(s.hostName)
   640→
   641→	for _, hit := range hits {
   642→		planBytes, err := json.Marshal(childAddressToPlanMap[hit.childAddress])
   643→		if err != nil {
   644→			s.logger.Error("Error marshaling plan data to JSON", zap.Error(err))
   645→		}
   646→		planString := string(planBytes)
   647→
   648→		s.lb.RecordDbServerTopQueryEvent(context.Background(),
   649→			timestamp,
   650→			dbSystemNameVal,
   651→			s.hostName,
   652→			hit.queryText,
   653→			planString, hit.sqlID, hit.childNumber,
   654→			hit.childAddress,
   655→			asFloatInSeconds(hit.metrics[applicationWaitTimeMetric]),
   656→			hit.metrics[bufferGetsMetric],
   657→			asFloatInSeconds(hit.metrics[clusterWaitTimeMetric]),
   658→			asFloatInSeconds(hit.metrics[concurrencyWaitTimeMetric]),
   659→			asFloatInSeconds(hit.metrics[cpuTimeMetric]),
   660→			hit.metrics[queryDirectReadsMetric],
   661→			hit.metrics[queryDirectWritesMetric],
   662→			hit.metrics[queryDiskReadsMetric],
   663→			asFloatInSeconds(hit.metrics[elapsedTimeMetric]),
   664→			hit.metrics[queryExecutionMetric],
   665→			hit.metrics[physicalReadBytesMetric],
   666→			hit.metrics[physicalReadRequestsMetric],
   667→			hit.metrics[physicalWriteBytesMetric],
   668→			hit.metrics[physicalWriteRequestsMetric],
   669→			hit.metrics[rowsProcessedMetric],
   670→			asFloatInSeconds(hit.metrics[userIoWaitTimeMetric]))
   671→	}
   672→
   673→	hitCount := len(hits)
   674→	if hitCount > 0 {
   675→		s.logger.Debug("Log records for this scrape", zap.Int("count", hitCount))
   676→	}
   677→
   678→	s.lb.Emit(metadata.WithLogsResource(rb.Emit())).ResourceLogs().MoveAndAppendTo(logs.ResourceLogs())
   679→
   680→	return errors.Join(errs...)
   681→}
   682→
   683→func (s *oracleScraper) collectQuerySamples(ctx context.Context, logs plog.Logs) error {
   684→	const action = "ACTION"
   685→	const duration = "DURATION_SEC"
   686→	const event = "EVENT"
   687→	const hostName = "MACHINE"
   688→	const module = "MODULE"
   689→	const osUser = "OSUSER"
   690→	const objectName = "OBJECT_NAME"
   691→	const objectType = "OBJECT_TYPE"
   692→	const process = "PROCESS"
   693→	const program = "PROGRAM"
   694→	const planHashValue = "PLAN_HASH_VALUE"
   695→	const sqlID = "SQL_ID"
   696→	const schemaName = "SCHEMANAME"
   697→	const sqlChildNumber = "SQL_CHILD_NUMBER"
   698→	const sid = "SID"
   699→	const serialNumber = "SERIAL#"
   700→	const status = "STATUS"
   701→	const state = "STATE"
   702→	const sqlText = "SQL_FULLTEXT"
   703→	const username = "USERNAME"
   704→	const waitclass = "WAIT_CLASS"
   705→	const port = "PORT"
   706→	const serviceName = "SERVICE_NAME"
   707→
   708→	var scrapeErrors []error
   709→
   710→	dbClients := s.samplesQueryClient
   711→	propagator := propagation.TraceContext{}
   712→	timestamp := pcommon.NewTimestampFromTime(time.Now())
   713→
   714→	rows, err := dbClients.metricRows(ctx, s.querySampleCfg.MaxRowsPerQuery)
   715→	if err != nil {
   716→		scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("error executing %s: %w", samplesQuery, err))
   717→	}
   718→
   719→	rb := s.lb.NewResourceBuilder()
   720→	rb.SetOracledbInstanceName(s.instanceName)
   721→	rb.SetHostName(s.hostName)
   722→
   723→	for _, row := range rows {
   724→		if row[sqlText] == "" {
   725→			continue
   726→		}
   727→
   728→		obfuscatedSQL, err := s.obfuscator.obfuscateSQLString(row[sqlText])
   729→		if err != nil {
   730→			s.logger.Error(fmt.Sprintf("oracleScraper failed updating this log record: %s", err))
   731→			continue
   732→		}
   733→
   734→		queryPlanHashVal := hex.EncodeToString([]byte(row[planHashValue]))
   735→
   736→		queryDuration, err := strconv.ParseFloat(row[duration], 64)
   737→		if err != nil {
   738→			scrapeErrors = append(scrapeErrors, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse int64 for Duration, value was %s: %w", row[duration], err))
   739→		}
   740→
   741→		clientPort, err := strconv.ParseInt(row[port], 10, 64)
   742→		if err != nil {
   743→			clientPort = 0
   744→		}
   745→
   746→		queryContext := propagator.Extract(ctx, propagation.MapCarrier{
   747→			"traceparent": row[action],
   748→		})
   749→
   750→		s.lb.RecordDbServerQuerySampleEvent(queryContext, timestamp, obfuscatedSQL, dbSystemNameVal, row[username], row[serviceName], row[hostName],
   751→			clientPort, row[hostName], clientPort, queryPlanHashVal, row[sqlID], row[sqlChildNumber], row[sid], row[serialNumber], row[process],
   752→			row[schemaName], row[program], row[module], row[status], row[state], row[waitclass], row[event], row[objectName], row[objectType],
   753→			row[osUser], queryDuration)
   754→	}
   755→
   756→	s.lb.Emit(metadata.WithLogsResource(rb.Emit())).ResourceLogs().MoveAndAppendTo(logs.ResourceLogs())
   757→
   758→	return errors.Join(scrapeErrors...)
   759→}
   760→
   761→func asFloatInSeconds(value int64) float64 {
   762→	return float64(value) / 1_000_000
   763→}
   764→
   765→func (s *oracleScraper) obfuscateCacheHits(hits []queryMetricCacheHit) []queryMetricCacheHit {
   766→	var obfuscatedHits []queryMetricCacheHit
   767→	for _, hit := range hits {
   768→		// obfuscate and normalize the query text
   769→		obfuscatedSQL, err := s.obfuscator.obfuscateSQLString(hit.queryText)
   770→		if err != nil {
   771→			s.logger.Error("oracleScraper failed getting metric rows", zap.Error(err))
   772→		} else {
   773→			obfuscatedSQLLowerCase := strings.ToLower(obfuscatedSQL)
   774→			hit.queryText = obfuscatedSQLLowerCase
   775→			obfuscatedHits = append(obfuscatedHits, hit)
   776→		}
   777→	}
   778→	return obfuscatedHits
   779→}
   780→
   781→func (s *oracleScraper) getChildAddressToPlanMap(ctx context.Context, hits []queryMetricCacheHit) map[string][]metricRow {
   782→	childAddressToPlanMap := map[string][]metricRow{}
   783→	if len(hits) == 0 {
   784→		return childAddressToPlanMap
   785→	}
   786→
   787→	var childAddressSlice []any
   788→	placeholders := make([]string, len(hits))
   789→	for i, hit := range hits {
   790→		placeholders[i] = fmt.Sprintf("HEXTORAW(:%d)", i+1)
   791→		childAddressSlice = append(childAddressSlice, hit.childAddress)
   792→	}
   793→
   794→	placeholdersCombined := strings.Join(placeholders, ", ")
   795→	sqlQuery := fmt.Sprintf(oracleQueryPlanDataSQL, placeholdersCombined)
   796→
   797→	s.logger.Debug("Fetching execution plans")
   798→	s.oraclePlanDataClient = s.clientProviderFunc(s.db, sqlQuery, s.logger)
   799→	planData, _ := s.oraclePlanDataClient.metricRows(ctx, childAddressSlice...)
   800→
   801→	for _, row := range planData {
   802→		currentChildAddress := row[childAddressAttr]
   803→		jsonPlansSlice, ok := childAddressToPlanMap[currentChildAddress]
   804→		// child address was for internal use only, it's not going to be used beyond this point
   805→		delete(row, childAddressAttr)
   806→		if ok {
   807→			childAddressToPlanMap[currentChildAddress] = append(jsonPlansSlice, row)
   808→		} else {
   809→			childAddressToPlanMap[currentChildAddress] = []metricRow{row}
   810→		}
   811→	}
   812→
   813→	return childAddressToPlanMap
   814→}
   815→
   816→func (*oracleScraper) getTopNMetricNames() []string {
   817→	return []string{
   818→		elapsedTimeMetric, queryExecutionMetric, cpuTimeMetric, applicationWaitTimeMetric,
   819→		concurrencyWaitTimeMetric, userIoWaitTimeMetric, clusterWaitTimeMetric, rowsProcessedMetric, bufferGetsMetric,
   820→		physicalReadRequestsMetric, physicalWriteRequestsMetric, physicalReadBytesMetric, physicalWriteBytesMetric,
   821→		queryDirectReadsMetric, queryDirectWritesMetric, queryDiskReadsMetric,
   822→	}
   823→}
   824→
   825→func (s *oracleScraper) shutdown(_ context.Context) error {
   826→	if s.db == nil {
   827→		return nil
   828→	}
   829→	return s.db.Close()
   830→}
   831→

   178→      - oracledb.child_address
   179→      - oracledb.application_wait_time
   180→      - oracledb.buffer_gets
   181→      - oracledb.cluster_wait_time
   182→      - oracledb.concurrency_wait_time
   183→      - oracledb.cpu_time
   184→      - oracledb.direct_reads
   185→      - oracledb.direct_writes
   186→      - oracledb.disk_reads
   187→      - oracledb.elapsed_time
   188→      - oracledb.executions
   189→      - oracledb.physical_read_bytes
   190→      - oracledb.physical_read_requests
   191→      - oracledb.physical_write_bytes
   192→      - oracledb.physical_write_requests
   193→      - oracledb.rows_processed
   194→      - oracledb.user_io_wait_time
   195→  db.server.query_sample:
   196→    enabled: false
   197→    description: sample query
   198→    attributes:
   199→      - db.query.text
   200→      - db.system.name
   201→      - user.name
   202→      - db.namespace
   203→      - client.address
   204→      - client.port
   205→      - network.peer.address
   206→      - network.peer.port
   207→      - oracledb.plan_hash_value
   208→      - oracledb.sql_id
   209→      - oracledb.child_number
   210→      - oracledb.sid
   211→      - oracledb.serial
   212→      - oracledb.process
   213→      - oracledb.schemaname
   214→      - oracledb.program
   215→      - oracledb.module
   216→      - oracledb.status
   217→      - oracledb.state
   218→      - oracledb.wait_class
   219→      - oracledb.event
   220→      - oracledb.object_name
   221→      - oracledb.object_type
   222→      - oracledb.osuser
   223→      - oracledb.duration_sec
   224→metrics:
   225→  oracledb.cpu_time:
   226→    description: Cumulative CPU time, in seconds
   227→    enabled: true
   228→    sum:
   229→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   230→      monotonic: true
   231→      value_type: double
   232→    unit: s
   233→  oracledb.enqueue_deadlocks:
   234→    description: Total number of deadlocks between table or row locks in different
   235→      sessions.
   236→    enabled: true
   237→    sum:
   238→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   239→      monotonic: true
   240→      value_type: int
   241→      input_type: string
   242→    unit: "{deadlocks}"
   243→  oracledb.exchange_deadlocks:
   244→    description: Number of times that a process detected a potential deadlock when
   245→      exchanging two buffers and raised an internal, restartable error. Index scans
   246→      are the only operations that perform exchanges.
   247→    enabled: true
   248→    sum:
   249→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   250→      monotonic: true
   251→      value_type: int
   252→      input_type: string
   253→    unit: "{deadlocks}"
   254→  oracledb.executions:
   255→    description: Total number of calls (user and recursive) that executed SQL statements
   256→    enabled: true
   257→    sum:
   258→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   259→      monotonic: true
   260→      value_type: int
   261→      input_type: string
   262→    unit: "{executions}"
   263→  oracledb.logical_reads:
   264→    description: Number of logical reads
   265→    enabled: true
   266→    sum:
   267→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   268→      monotonic: true
   269→      value_type: int
   270→      input_type: string
   271→    unit: "{reads}"
   272→  oracledb.hard_parses:
   273→    description: Number of hard parses
   274→    enabled: true
   275→    sum:
   276→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   277→      monotonic: true
   278→      value_type: int
   279→      input_type: string
   280→    unit: "{parses}"
   281→  oracledb.parse_calls:
   282→    description: Total number of parse calls.
   283→    enabled: true
   284→    sum:
   285→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   286→      monotonic: true
   287→      value_type: int
   288→      input_type: string
   289→    unit: "{parses}"
   290→  oracledb.pga_memory:
   291→    description: Session PGA (Program Global Area) memory
   292→    enabled: true
   293→    sum:
   294→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   295→      monotonic: true
   296→      value_type: int
   297→      input_type: string
   298→    unit: By
   299→  oracledb.physical_reads:
   300→    description: Number of physical reads
   301→    enabled: true
   302→    sum:
   303→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   304→      monotonic: true
   305→      value_type: int
   306→      input_type: string
   307→    unit: "{reads}"
   308→  oracledb.physical_reads_direct:
   309→    description: Number of reads directly from disk, bypassing the buffer cache
   310→    enabled: false
   311→    sum:
   312→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   313→      monotonic: true
   314→      value_type: int
   315→      input_type: string
   316→    unit: "{reads}"
   317→  oracledb.physical_read_io_requests:
   318→    description: Number of read requests for application activity
   319→    enabled: false
   320→    sum:
   321→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   322→      monotonic: true
   323→      value_type: int
   324→      input_type: string
   325→    unit: "{requests}"
   326→  oracledb.physical_writes:
   327→    description: Number of physical writes
   328→    enabled: false
   329→    sum:
   330→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   331→      monotonic: true
   332→      value_type: int
   333→      input_type: string
   334→    unit: "{writes}"
   335→  oracledb.physical_writes_direct:
   336→    description: Number of writes directly to disk, bypassing the buffer cache
   337→    enabled: false
   338→    sum:
   339→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   340→      monotonic: true
   341→      value_type: int
   342→      input_type: string
   343→    unit: "{writes}"
   344→  oracledb.physical_write_io_requests:
   345→    description: Number of write requests for application activity
   346→    enabled: false
   347→    sum:
   348→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   349→      monotonic: true
   350→      value_type: int
   351→      input_type: string
   352→    unit: "{requests}"
   353→  oracledb.user_commits:
   354→    description: Number of user commits. When a user commits a transaction, the redo
   355→      generated that reflects the changes made to database blocks must be written
   356→      to disk. Commits often represent the closest thing to a user transaction rate.
   357→    enabled: true
   358→    sum:
   359→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   360→      monotonic: true
   361→      value_type: int
   362→      input_type: string
   363→    unit: "{commits}"
   364→  oracledb.user_rollbacks:
   365→    description: Number of times users manually issue the ROLLBACK statement or an
   366→      error occurs during a user's transactions
   367→    enabled: true
   368→    sum:
   369→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   370→      monotonic: true
   371→      value_type: int
   372→      input_type: string
   373→    unit: "1"
   374→  oracledb.sessions.usage:
   375→    attributes:
   376→      - session_type
   377→      - session_status
   378→    description: Count of active sessions.
   379→    enabled: true
   380→    gauge:
   381→      value_type: int
   382→      input_type: string
   383→    unit: "{sessions}"
   384→  oracledb.processes.usage:
   385→    description: Current count of active processes.
   386→    enabled: true
   387→    gauge:
   388→      value_type: int
   389→      input_type: string
   390→    unit: "{processes}"
   391→  oracledb.processes.limit:
   392→    description: Maximum limit of active processes, -1 if unlimited.
   393→    enabled: true
   394→    gauge:
   395→      value_type: int
   396→      input_type: string
   397→    unit: "{processes}"
   398→  oracledb.sessions.limit:
   399→    description: Maximum limit of active sessions, -1 if unlimited.
   400→    enabled: true
   401→    gauge:
   402→      value_type: int
   403→      input_type: string
   404→    unit: "{sessions}"
   405→  oracledb.enqueue_locks.usage:
   406→    description: Current count of active enqueue locks.
   407→    enabled: true
   408→    gauge:
   409→      value_type: int
   410→      input_type: string
   411→    unit: "{locks}"
   412→  oracledb.enqueue_locks.limit:
   413→    description: Maximum limit of active enqueue locks, -1 if unlimited.
   414→    enabled: true
   415→    gauge:
   416→      value_type: int
   417→      input_type: string
   418→    unit: "{locks}"
   419→  oracledb.dml_locks.usage:
   420→    description: Current count of active DML (Data Manipulation Language) locks.
   421→    enabled: true
   422→    gauge:
   423→      value_type: int
   424→      input_type: string
   425→    unit: "{locks}"
   426→  oracledb.dml_locks.limit:
   427→    description: Maximum limit of active DML (Data Manipulation Language) locks, -1 if unlimited.
   428→    enabled: true
   429→    gauge:
   430→      value_type: int
   431→      input_type: string
   432→    unit: "{locks}"
   433→  oracledb.enqueue_resources.usage:
   434→    description: Current count of active enqueue resources.
   435→    enabled: true
   436→    gauge:
   437→      value_type: int
   438→      input_type: string
   439→    unit: "{resources}"
   440→  oracledb.enqueue_resources.limit:
   441→    description: Maximum limit of active enqueue resources, -1 if unlimited.
   442→    enabled: true
   443→    gauge:
   444→      value_type: int
   445→      input_type: string
   446→    unit: "{resources}"
   447→  oracledb.transactions.usage:
   448→    description: Current count of active transactions.
   449→    enabled: true
   450→    gauge:
   451→      value_type: int
   452→      input_type: string
   453→    unit: "{transactions}"
   454→  oracledb.transactions.limit:
   455→    description: Maximum limit of active transactions, -1 if unlimited.
   456→    enabled: true
   457→    gauge:
   458→      value_type: int
   459→      input_type: string
   460→    unit: "{transactions}"
   461→  oracledb.tablespace_size.limit:
   462→    attributes:
   463→      - tablespace_name
   464→    description: Maximum size of tablespace in bytes, -1 if unlimited.
   465→    enabled: true
   466→    gauge:
   467→      value_type: int
   468→    unit: By
   469→  oracledb.tablespace_size.usage:
   470→    attributes:
   471→      - tablespace_name
   472→    description: Used tablespace in bytes.
   473→    enabled: true
   474→    gauge:
   475→      value_type: int
   476→    unit: By
   477→  oracledb.db_block_gets:
   478→    description: Number of times a current block was requested from the buffer cache.
   479→    enabled: false
   480→    sum:
   481→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   482→      monotonic: true
   483→      value_type: int
   484→      input_type: string
   485→    unit: "{gets}"
   486→  oracledb.consistent_gets:
   487→    description: Number of times a consistent read was requested for a block from the buffer cache.
   488→    enabled: false
   489→    sum:
   490→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   491→      monotonic: true
   492→      value_type: int
   493→      input_type: string
   494→    unit: "{gets}"
   495→  oracledb.queries_parallelized:
   496→    description: Number of SELECT statements executed in parallel
   497→    enabled: false
   498→    sum:
   499→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   500→      monotonic: true
   501→      value_type: int
   502→      input_type: string
   503→    unit: "{queries}"
   504→  oracledb.ddl_statements_parallelized:
   505→    description: Number of DDL statements that were executed in parallel
   506→    enabled: false
   507→    sum:
   508→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   509→      monotonic: true
   510→      value_type: int
   511→      input_type: string
   512→    unit: "{statements}"
   513→  oracledb.dml_statements_parallelized:
   514→    description: Number of DML statements that were executed in parallel
   515→    enabled: false
   516→    sum:
   517→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   518→      monotonic: true
   519→      value_type: int
   520→      input_type: string
   521→    unit: "{statements}"
   522→  oracledb.parallel_operations_not_downgraded:
   523→    description: Number of times parallel execution was executed at the
   524→      requested degree of parallelism
   525→    enabled: false
   526→    sum:
   527→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   528→      monotonic: true
   529→      value_type: int
   530→      input_type: string
   531→    unit: "{executions}"
   532→  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_to_serial:
   533→    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested but execution
   534→      was serial because of insufficient parallel execution servers
   535→    enabled: false
   536→    sum:
   537→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   538→      monotonic: true
   539→      value_type: int
   540→      input_type: string
   541→    unit: "{executions}"
   542→  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_1_to_25_pct:
   543→    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the
   544→      degree of parallelism was reduced down to 1-25% because of insufficient
   545→      parallel execution servers
   546→    enabled: false
   547→    sum:
   548→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   549→      monotonic: true
   550→      value_type: int
   551→      input_type: string
   552→    unit: "{executions}"
   553→  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_25_to_50_pct:
   554→    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the
   555→      degree of parallelism was reduced down to 25-50% because of insufficient
   556→      parallel execution servers
   557→    enabled: false
   558→    sum:
   559→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   560→      monotonic: true
   561→      value_type: int
   562→      input_type: string
   563→    unit: "{executions}"
   564→  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_50_to_75_pct:
   565→    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the
   566→      degree of parallelism was reduced down to 50-75% because of insufficient
   567→      parallel execution servers
   568→    enabled: false
   569→    sum:
   570→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   571→      monotonic: true
   572→      value_type: int
   573→      input_type: string
   574→    unit: "{executions}"
   575→  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_75_to_99_pct:
   576→    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the
   577→      degree of parallelism was reduced down to 75-99% because of insufficient
   578→      parallel execution servers
   579→    enabled: false
   580→    sum:
   581→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   582→      monotonic: true
   583→      value_type: int
   584→      input_type: string
   585→    unit: "{executions}"
   586→  oracledb.logons:
   587→    description: Number of logon operations
   588→    enabled: false
   589→    sum:
   590→      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
   591→      monotonic: true
   592→      value_type: int
   593→      input_type: string
   594→    unit: "{operation}"
   595→
   596→
   597→tests:
   598→  config:
   599→  goleak:
   600→    ignore:
   601→      any:
   602→      - "github.com/cihub/seelog.(*asyncLoopLogger).processQueue" # see https://github.com/cihub/seelog/issues/182

file.content:
type: oracledb

status:
  class: receiver
  stability:
    alpha: [metrics]
    development: [logs]
  distributions: [contrib]
  codeowners:
    active: [dmitryax, crobert-1, atoulme]

resource_attributes:
  oracledb.instance.name:
    description: The name of the instance that data is coming from.
    enabled: true
    type: string
  host.name:
    description: The host name of Oracle Server
    enabled: true
    type: string

attributes:
  session_status:
    description: Session status
    type: string
  session_type:
    description: Session type
    type: string
  tablespace_name:
    description: Tablespace name
    type: string
  # attributes for events
  ## top query
  db.query.text:
    description: The text of the database query being executed.
    type: string
  oracledb.query_plan:
    description: The query execution plan used by the SQL Server.
    type: string
  oracledb.sql_id:
    description: The SQL ID of the query.
    type: string
  oracledb.child_number:
    description: The child number of the query.
    type: string
  oracledb.child_address:
    description: Address of the child cursor.
    type: string
  oracledb.application_wait_time:
    description: The total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting on the application before it could proceed with execution (reporting delta).
    type: double
  oracledb.buffer_gets:
    description: Number of logical reads (i.e., buffer cache accesses) performed by a query (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.cluster_wait_time:
    description: Total time (in seconds) that a query waited due to Oracle Real Application Clusters (RAC) coordination (reporting delta).
    type: double
  oracledb.concurrency_wait_time:
    description: Total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting on concurrency-related events (reporting delta).
    type: double
  oracledb.cpu_time:
    description: Total time (in seconds) that the CPU spent actively processing a query, excluding time spent waiting (reporting delta).
    type: double
  oracledb.direct_reads:
    description: The number of direct path reads performed by a query — i.e., data blocks read directly from disk into the session’s memory (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.direct_writes:
    description: The number of direct path write operations, where data is written directly to disk from user memory (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.disk_reads:
    description: The number of physical reads a query performs — that is, the number of data blocks read from disk (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.elapsed_time:
    description: The total time (in seconds) taken by a query from start to finish, including CPU time and all types of waits (reporting delta).
    type: double
  oracledb.executions:
    description: The number of times a specific SQL query has been executed (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.physical_read_bytes:
    description: The total number of bytes read from disk by a query (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.physical_read_requests:
    description: The number of physical I/O read operations performed by a query (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.physical_write_bytes:
    description: The total number of bytes written to disk by a query (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.physical_write_requests:
    description: The number of times a query requested to write data to disk (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.rows_processed:
    description: The total number of rows that a query has read, returned, or affected during its execution (reporting delta).
    type: int
  oracledb.user_io_wait_time:
    description: The total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting for user I/O operations—such as reading or writing data to disk or network file systems (reporting delta).
    type: double
  db.server.name:
    description: The name of the server hosting the database.
    type: string
  db.system.name:
    description: The database management system (DBMS) product as identified by the client instrumentation.
    type: string
  oracledb.plan_hash_value:
    description: Binary hash value calculated on the query execution plan and used to identify similar query execution plans, reported in the HEX format.
    type: string
  client.address:
    description: Hostname or address of the client.
    type: string
  client.port:
    description: TCP port used by the client.
    type: int
  db.namespace:
    description: The database name.
    type: string
  network.peer.address:
    description: IP address of the peer client.
    type: string
  network.peer.port:
    description: TCP port used by the peer client.
    type: int
  oracledb.sid:
    description: ID of the Oracle Server session.
    type: string
  oracledb.serial:
    description: Serial number associated with a session.
    type: string
  oracledb.process:
    description: The operating system process ID (PID) associated with a session.
    type: string
  user.name:
    description: Database user name under which a session is connected to
    type: string
  oracledb.schemaname:
    description: Oracle schema under which SQL statements are being executed
    type: string
  oracledb.program:
    description: Name of the client program or tool that initiated the Oracle database session.
    type: string
  oracledb.module:
    description: Logical module name of the client application that initiated a query or session.
    type: string
  oracledb.status:
    description: Execution state or result of a database query or session.
    type: string
  oracledb.state:
    description: Current state of the query or the session executing it.
    type: string
  oracledb.wait_class:
    description: The category of wait events a query or session is currently experiencing in Oracle Database.
    type: string
  oracledb.event:
    description: The specific wait event that a query or session is currently experiencing.
    type: string
  oracledb.object_name:
    description: Name of the database object that a query is accessing.
    type: string
  oracledb.object_type:
    description: Type of the database object that a query is accessing.
    type: string
  oracledb.osuser:
    description: Name of the operating system user that initiated or is running the Oracle database session.
    type: string
  oracledb.duration_sec:
    description: Total time taken by a database query to execute.
    type: double

events:
  db.server.top_query:
    enabled: false
    description: Collection of event metrics for top N queries, filtered based on the highest CPU time consumed (oracledb.elapsed_time).
    attributes:
      - db.system.name
      - db.server.name
      - db.query.text
      - oracledb.query_plan
      - oracledb.sql_id
      - oracledb.child_number
      - oracledb.child_address
      - oracledb.application_wait_time
      - oracledb.buffer_gets
      - oracledb.cluster_wait_time
      - oracledb.concurrency_wait_time
      - oracledb.cpu_time
      - oracledb.direct_reads
      - oracledb.direct_writes
      - oracledb.disk_reads
      - oracledb.elapsed_time
      - oracledb.executions
      - oracledb.physical_read_bytes
      - oracledb.physical_read_requests
      - oracledb.physical_write_bytes
      - oracledb.physical_write_requests
      - oracledb.rows_processed
      - oracledb.user_io_wait_time
  db.server.query_sample:
    enabled: false
    description: sample query
    attributes:
      - db.query.text
      - db.system.name
      - user.name
      - db.namespace
      - client.address
      - client.port
      - network.peer.address
      - network.peer.port
      - oracledb.plan_hash_value
      - oracledb.sql_id
      - oracledb.child_number
      - oracledb.sid
      - oracledb.serial
      - oracledb.process
      - oracledb.schemaname
      - oracledb.program
      - oracledb.module
      - oracledb.status
      - oracledb.state
      - oracledb.wait_class
      - oracledb.event
      - oracledb.object_name
      - oracledb.object_type
      - oracledb.osuser
      - oracledb.duration_sec
metrics:
  oracledb.cpu_time:
    description: Cumulative CPU time, in seconds
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: double
    unit: s
  oracledb.enqueue_deadlocks:
    description: Total number of deadlocks between table or row locks in different
      sessions.
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{deadlocks}"
  oracledb.exchange_deadlocks:
    description: Number of times that a process detected a potential deadlock when
      exchanging two buffers and raised an internal, restartable error. Index scans
      are the only operations that perform exchanges.
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{deadlocks}"
  oracledb.executions:
    description: Total number of calls (user and recursive) that executed SQL statements
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{executions}"
  oracledb.logical_reads:
    description: Number of logical reads
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{reads}"
  oracledb.hard_parses:
    description: Number of hard parses
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{parses}"
  oracledb.parse_calls:
    description: Total number of parse calls.
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{parses}"
  oracledb.pga_memory:

      monotonic: true

      input_type: string
    unit: By
  oracledb.physical_reads:
    description: Number of physical reads
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{reads}"
  oracledb.physical_reads_direct:
    description: Number of reads directly from disk, bypassing the buffer cache
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{reads}"
  oracledb.physical_read_io_requests:
    description: Number of read requests for application activity
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{requests}"
  oracledb.physical_writes:
    description: Number of physical writes
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{writes}"
  oracledb.physical_writes_direct:
    description: Number of writes directly to disk, bypassing the buffer cache
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{writes}"
  oracledb.physical_write_io_requests:
    description: Number of write requests for application activity
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{requests}"
  oracledb.user_commits:
    description: Number of user commits. When a user commits a transaction, the redo
      generated that reflects the changes made to database blocks must be written
      to disk. Commits often represent the closest thing to a user transaction rate.
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{commits}"
  oracledb.user_rollbacks:
    description: Number of times users manually issue the ROLLBACK statement or an
      error occurs during a user's transactions
    enabled: true
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "1"
  oracledb.sessions.usage:
    attributes:
      - session_type
      - session_status
    description: Count of active sessions.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{sessions}"
  oracledb.processes.usage:
    description: Current count of active processes.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{processes}"
  oracledb.processes.limit:

    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{processes}"
  oracledb.sessions.limit:
    description: Maximum limit of active sessions, -1 if unlimited.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{sessions}"
  oracledb.enqueue_locks.usage:
    description: Current count of active enqueue locks.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{locks}"
  oracledb.enqueue_locks.limit:
    description: Maximum limit of active enqueue locks, -1 if unlimited.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{locks}"
  oracledb.dml_locks.usage:
    description: Current count of active DML (Data Manipulation Language) locks.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{locks}"
  oracledb.dml_locks.limit:
    description: Maximum limit of active DML (Data Manipulation Language) locks, -1 if unlimited.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{locks}"
  oracledb.enqueue_resources.usage:
    description: Current count of active enqueue resources.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{resources}"
  oracledb.enqueue_resources.limit:
    description: Maximum limit of active enqueue resources, -1 if unlimited.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{resources}"
  oracledb.transactions.usage:
    description: Current count of active transactions.
    enabled: true
    gauge:

      input_type: string
    unit: "{transactions}"
  oracledb.transactions.limit:
    description: Maximum limit of active transactions, -1 if unlimited.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{transactions}"
  oracledb.tablespace_size.limit:

      - tablespace_name

    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
    unit: By
  oracledb.tablespace_size.usage:
    attributes:
      - tablespace_name
    description: Used tablespace in bytes.
    enabled: true
    gauge:
      value_type: int
    unit: By
  oracledb.db_block_gets:
    description: Number of times a current block was requested from the buffer cache.
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{gets}"
  oracledb.consistent_gets:
    description: Number of times a consistent read was requested for a block from the buffer cache.
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{gets}"
  oracledb.queries_parallelized:
    description: Number of SELECT statements executed in parallel
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{queries}"
  oracledb.ddl_statements_parallelized:
    description: Number of DDL statements that were executed in parallel
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{statements}"
  oracledb.dml_statements_parallelized:
    description: Number of DML statements that were executed in parallel
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{statements}"
  oracledb.parallel_operations_not_downgraded:

      requested degree of parallelism
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true

      input_type: string
    unit: "{executions}"
  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_to_serial:
    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested but execution

    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{executions}"
  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_1_to_25_pct:
    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the

      parallel execution servers
    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{executions}"
  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_25_to_50_pct:
    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the

    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true

    unit: "{executions}"
  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_50_to_75_pct:
    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the

    enabled: false
    sum:
      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
      value_type: int
      input_type: string
    unit: "{executions}"
  oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_75_to_99_pct:
    description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the

    enabled: false

      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true

    unit: "{executions}"

    description: Number of logon operations

      aggregation_temporality: cumulative
      monotonic: true
